_comment: Representative chunks paraphrased from aerotech-aeroscript style docs. Captures the patterns the reviewer cited as producing junk: sentence-leading capitalized English words (When, Each, Returns, Values), trailing-period mentions (AxisFault., HomeType.), single-char and short prose, and real declared types interleaved with prose.
chunks: Use the MoveLinear command to perform a coordinated linear motion. Each axis in the group moves at the same time. When all axes finish, the next program statement runs.
AxisFault is the enumeration that lists fault conditions on an axis. Refer to the AxisFault. enumeration for the complete list of values. Set AxisFault.Disabled to acknowledge a latched fault.
enum HomeType { ToLimit, ToMarker, ToMarkerOnly, ToLimitAndMarker } The HomeType parameter selects which homing routine the controller runs. Returns the enumeration value used during homing.
Set the RampMode parameter to control acceleration shape. Valid values are RampMode.Linear, RampMode.SCurve, and RampMode.SineSquared. Each ramp shape produces a distinct motion profile.
class Controller { public Status GetAxisStatus(int axisNumber); public void Connect(string ipAddress); }  AxisStatus represents the runtime state of a single axis.
function MoveLinear(axis, distance, velocity) returns Status. Represents the lowest-level motion call. The function blocks until the move completes or an AxisFault is raised.
enum ServoLoopGain { Position, Velocity, Acceleration }  ServoLoopGain identifies which control loop a parameter affects. When tuning, adjust ServoLoopGain.Velocity first.
TaskState describes the runtime state of a task: Inactive, Running, Paused, or Stopped. Use the TaskState. enumeration to compare task state in a guard expression.
Each AnalogOutputUpdateEvent fires when the controller writes a value to an analog output channel. Use the event to log values or trigger conditional logic.
For more information, see the section on Coordinated Motion. The system supports up to 32 axes per task.
